clue: The 1896 Anglo-Zanzibar War is the shortest war in recorded history, most often cited as lasting only ___ minutes.
In Boston's 1919 Great Molasses Flood, a burst tank sent a wave of molasses tearing through the streets at an estimated ___ miles per hour.
During Australia's 1932 Great Emu War, soldiers with machine guns were issued 10,000 rounds of ammunition to fight flocks of ___, and largely lost.
To dispose of a rotting whale carcass in 1970, the Oregon Highway Division blew it up using about half a ton of ___.
The 1325 War of the Bucket between Modena and Bologna is named for a trophy the victors carried off: a wooden ___ taken from a well.
London's deadly 1814 Beer Flood, which killed eight people, was unleashed when a giant brewery vat of ___ burst open.
At Spain's El Colacho festival, men costumed as the Devil leap over rows of recently born ___ laid on mattresses in the street.
Oaxaca's Night of the Radishes, a competition to carve elaborate scenes out of oversized radishes, became a formal city event in the year ___.
At Ramsbottom's World Black Pudding Throwing Championships, competitors hurl black puddings to knock a stack of ___ off a tall plinth.
The winner of the Wife Carrying World Championship in Sonkajarvi, Finland famously receives a prize equal to the carried partner's weight in ___.
At Gawthorpe's World Coal Carrying Championship in Yorkshire, men race about a kilometre uphill while lugging a sack of coal weighing ___ kilograms.
Competitors at the World Stone Skimming Championships on Scotland's Easdale Island may only skim stones made of ___.
At the 2024 World Conker Championships, an 82-year-old champion was investigated for cheating after a ___ conker was found in his pocket.
In Bavaria's Fingerhakeln championship, two seated men each hook a single ___ into a leather strap and try to yank each other across a table.
At Egremont's centuries-old Crab Fair in Cumbria, the World Gurning Championship is won by pulling the ugliest face while framed by a horse collar known as a ___.
